senses.definition: to hurry or move with quick, short steps; to scurry or bustle about
examples: The children scuddled across the yard to avoid getting wet.
He scuddled through the crowd to reach the exit.

senses.definition: the past tense of scuddle; moved quickly and lightly in a hurried manner
examples: The dog scuddled away when the door opened.
She scuddled to the bus stop just in time.

senses.definition: scuddles is the plural of scuddle, referring to quick, short movements or hurried strides
examples: The scuddles of tiny feet filled the hallway.
We watched the scuddles of ducks across the water.

senses.definition: the act of moving quickly and lightly; rushing about
examples: The kittens were scuddling around the room.
People scuddling through the station tried not to miss their trains.

senses.definition: characterized by or resembling scudding; quick and light in movement
examples: The scuddy birds skimmed the shoreline.
They kept up a scuddy pace to finish on time.

senses.definition: plural of scudo, a former Italian or medieval coin or monetary unit; historically used as currency
examples: In historical texts, merchants traded sums in scudi.
The ancient scroll mentions a scudi as payment.

senses.definition: a person who scuds; one who moves quickly or scampers
examples: The scudlers dodged through the market crowd.
A scudler darted past the fence.

senses.definition: plural of scudler; multiple quick movers
examples: The park was full of scudlers jumping over benches.
Scudlers weaved through the crowd.

senses.definition: scudo is a singular form of an old Italian coin or shield; used historically in finance or heraldry
examples: Ancient manuscripts mention the scudo as a unit of value.
The knight bore a gold scudo on the shield.

senses.definition: plural of scud; light, fast-moving clouds or fast gunfire projectiles in nautical or military contexts
examples: The ship rode the waves as scuds of rain hit the deck.
During the storm, scuds swept over the valley.

senses.definition: third person singular present of scud; to move fast in a straight line
examples: The cloud scuds across the sky.
The bomber scuds toward the target.

senses.definition: to scratch or scrape a surface lightly; to rub against something to wear away the finish
examples: The shoes scuffed the floor as he walked.
Don't scuff the paint on the cabinet.

senses.definition: a mark caused by scuffing; a worn or scratched area
examples: There are scuffs on the heel of the shoe.
The scuff on the door frame is hard to remove.

senses.definition: having marks or scratches from scuffing
examples: His scuffed sneakers showed signs of wear.
The book’s cover was scuffed but still intact.

senses.definition: a person or thing that scuffs or causes scuff marks; a tool used to scuff surfaces
examples: The scuffer on the mower wore down after years of use.
He nicknamed the old scuffer as the 'floor singer'.

senses.definition: plural of scuffer; devices or people that scuff surfaces
examples: The scuffers contributed to the worn floor.
Two scuffers were found near the boat dock.

senses.definition: the act of scuffing; the process of making scuff marks
examples: Scuffing can ruin the finish of the furniture.
Regular cleaning helps prevent scuffing.

senses.definition: a short, confused struggle or fight
examples: There was a scuffle in the crowd after the game.
The scuffle ended when security intervened.

senses.definition: to move in a disorderly way or engage in a brief fight
examples: Two fans scuffled at the doorway.
They scuffled to grab the last ticket.

senses.definition: past tense of scuffle; moved in a disorderly way or fought briefly
examples: They scuffled to board the bus.
The protesters scuffled with police at the gates.

senses.definition: a device or person that scuffs; something that creates scuff marks
examples: The old scuffler on the floor polishes away marks.
As a joke, someone called the shoes a 'scuffler'.

senses.definition: plural of scuffler; objects or people that cause scuffing
examples: The scufflers in the workshop wore protective shoes.
Two scufflers were observed near the gym floor.

senses.definition: plural of scuffle; short, chaotic struggles or fights
examples: There were several scuffles during the demonstration.
The scuffles were quickly controlled.

senses.definition: third person singular present of scuffle; to engage in short, chaotic struggles
examples: Fans scuffles with security at the gate.
The dogs scuffle over the bone.

senses.definition: the act of scuffling; chaotic, quick fighting or moving
examples: A scuffling broke out near the entrance.
The teams were involved in a scuffling for the ball.

senses.definition: characterized by scuffling; involved in quick, chaotic movement
examples: The players were scuffling teams for position.
A scuffling crowd gathered at the gate.

senses.definition: in a scuffling manner; in a disorderly, hurried way
examples: They moved scufflingly to avoid attention.
She ducked scufflingly through the crowd.

senses.definition: in a scuffling manner; in a quick, disorderly way
examples: They moved scufflingly to the exit.
The crowd pushed scufflingly through the gate.

senses.definition: characterized by scuffing; marked by scratches or scuffs
examples: The scuffly shoes looked worn but sturdy.
A scuffly surface required cleaning.

senses.definition: plural of scuff; marks or scratches on a surface
examples: There are several scuffs on the table.
You can buff out the scuffs with polish.

senses.definition: third person singular present of scuff; to cause scuffs
examples: The child scuffs the wall with a toy.
Her shoes scuff the floor as she walks.

senses.definition: having a rough, scuffed, or unpolished appearance
examples: The scuffy boots looked well worn.
They lived in a scuffy apartment but it was cozy.
